description: Playful absurdist jokes about impossible meeting venues lead into a genuine sprint planning scheduling request. The pivot from jokes to a real event is explicitly marked with 'joking aside'.

events.title: Sprint Planning
events.reasoning: After joking about absurd meeting locations, Alice pivots with 'joking aside' and makes a genuine request to lock in Wednesday at ten for sprint planning. Bob confirms and commits to sending an invite that afternoon. The big conference room is specified for whiteboard space.
events.assumptions: Wednesday = Feb 25; whole team present but only Alice and Bob confirmed by name; 1-hour default for sprint planning

mock_llm_response.summary: One event extracted: sprint planning on Wednesday Feb 25 at 10:00 AM in the big conference room. The absurdist joke meeting venues (ocean, hot air balloon, gondola) are correctly ignored.

notes: Joke then serious pattern. The explicit pivot phrase 'joking aside' clearly demarcates non-serious from serious intent. The model should recognize this transition signal and extract only the post-pivot event.
